==Biography==
Joshua "'''Chunkyfresh'''" Kesrawani is from Las Vegas, NV.  He qualified for the [[Riot League Championship Series/North America/2015 Season/Spring Expansion|NA Spring Expansion Tournament]] via the [[Riot League Championship Series/North America/2015 Season/Expansion/Challenger Ladder|ranked 5's ladder]] with the team [[Team Confusion eSports]] as their midlaner, but the team forfeited in the first round.<ref>[http://www.reddit.com/r/leagueoflegends/comments/2mkwbh/clarification_on_confusion_vs_fusion_forfeit/ Clarification on Confusion vs. Fusion forfeit (Fusion perspective)] ''reddit.com''</ref> Chunkyfresh was announced as one of the substitute players for [[Team Fusion]] when the roster was first assembled, and he was a top-10 Challenger soloqueue player at the time.<ref>[https://twitter.com/TeamFusiongg/status/525398680002916352 Team Fusion's Tweet] ''twitter.com''</ref><ref>[https://twitter.com/BeeSinLoL/status/525397524702826497 Bee Sin's Tweet] ''twitter.com''</ref>

When starting Fusion support [[Gleeb]] needed to take some time off from playing to adjust to a new medication, Chunkyfresh substituted for him in the Expansion Tournament, though Gleeb officially retained his starting position on the team.<ref>[http://teamfusion.gg/team-fusion-parts-ways-noblesse/ Team Fusion parts ways with Noblesse] ''teamfusion.gg''</ref> Fusion qualified for the online portion with Chunkyfresh and then started Gleeb for the offline portion but did not qualify for the LCS; however, their placing did qualify them for the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Spring Season|NACS Spring Season]].
===2015 Season===
Prior to the start of the season, both [[Maknoon]] and Gleeb left Fusion, and Chunkyfresh became their starting top laner while [[Trance]] replaced Gleeb. After a pattern of losing almost every off-stream game and winning every on-stream game, Fusion finished the NACS season in third place, with a 6-4 record, splitting 1-1 against every team except for [[CLG Black]]. In the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Spring Playoffs|playoffs]], Fusion lost to [[Team Dragon Knights]] and then beat [[Final Five]], finishing in third place. Despite Fusion's defeat by TDK, [[Winterfox]] chose to play against TDK in the [[Riot League Championship Series/North America/2015 Season/Summer Promotion|Promotion Tournament]], leaving Fusion to face [[Dignitas]] for a spot in the [[Riot League Championship Series/North America/2015 Season/Summer Season|summer LCS season]]. Fusion lost the series 2-3 but retained an automatic qualification into the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Summer Season|NACS Summer Season]]; however, the team disbanded.<ref>[https://twitter.com/fusionesportsgg/status/598543458253414400 Team Fusion's Tweet] ''twitter.com''</ref>

Chunkyfresh joined [[Odyssey Gaming]] along with fellow former Fusion player Trance in early May. After entering the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Summer Qualifier|NACS Summer Qualifier]] with the top [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Summer Qualifier/Ladder|ladder]] seed, they were eliminated in the second round of their bracket by [[Cloud9 Tempest]]. After the second week of the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Summer Season|summer season]], however, Chunkyfresh joined [[Team Imagine]] on loan from Odyssey. After finishing the season with a 6-4 record and in third place, Imagine officially merged with Odyssey under Imagine's name.<ref>[http://www.dailydot.com/esports/imagine-odyssey-merger/ Imagine and Odyssey merge, add Leviathan Dota 2 squad] ''dailydot.com''</ref><ref>[https://twitter.com/MaelstromGG/status/629730531945525248 Team Imagine's Tweet] ''twitter.com''</ref>

In the 2015 NACS [[2015_NA_Challenger_Series/Summer_Playoffs|Summer Playoffs]], Imagine lost in the semifinals to eventual tournament winner [[Renegades]], but won the third place match against [[Cloud 9 Tempest]] to secure a spot in the [[Riot_League_Championship_Series/North_America/2016_Season/Spring_Promotion|2016 LCS Promotion Tournament]]. Chosen by [[Team 8]], they lost 3-1 - out of [[League Championship Series/North America/2016 Season/Spring Promotion|LCS]] qualification, but regardless retaining their [[2016 NA Challenger Series/Spring Season|2016 NACS spring season]] placement.

== Trivia ==
* Became recognized for his skills playing {{ci|Gragas}}, leading many friends and fans to dub him the "Gragas God." <ref> CLG HotshotGG vs Chunkyfresh (Challenger I) http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qO_dwD4ki2U </ref>
